acceptance_criteria.description: npm run build exits 0 with zero TypeScript errors
node tools/validate-contracts.mjs exits 0 — chainless scan + public-safe scan both PASS
Impeccable critique ≥ 7/10 (Nielsen), 0 P0 findings on collector-facing surface
Hero copy is exactly 'Own the edition. Skip the wallet.' (baseline lock)
Primary CTA is 'Add to collection' (baseline lock)
Footer nonclaim present on every page: 'Vitrine collectibles are synthetic — for the collecting experience, not real ownership, transfer, or financial value.'
First-purchase notice fires before first Add to collection: 'A quick note: this is a synthetic collectible. You\'re collecting the experience — there\'s no real ownership, resale, or value.'
Negative code proof: grep of apps/nft-storefront/ for all forbidden_terms in nft-chainless.json returns 0 non-allowlisted matches
Negative dep proof: no wallet/chain/crypto package in package.json or node_modules/.package-lock.json
Local ownership fixture (lib/collection.tsx) stores to localStorage, never calls any chain RPC
All 8 pages render without console errors: /, /editions/[id], /checkout/[id], /collection, /collection/return/[id], /maker, /about, not-found
Collect → shelf flow is clickable end to end: browse → detail → Add to collection → checkout → Your Vitrine shelf displays the edition

engineering_plan.stack: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3.4, Nunito (Google Fonts)

structure.app/: Next.js App Router pages (8 routes + not-found)

copy_overrides: app/page.tsx: hero = 'Own the edition. Skip the wallet.', sub = 'Numbered digital editions with clear provenance and a shelf you\'re proud of — no wallet, no gas, no speculation.'
components/AddToCollectionButton.tsx: CTA = 'Add to collection', owned state = 'In your Vitrine · no. X of Y'
engineering_plan.dynamic_segment_fix: Rename all -id- dirs to [id] (Next.js dynamic route syntax)
no_changes: All design tokens (globals.css, tailwind.config.ts) — verbatim from UI source
All components not listed in copy_overrides — verbatim
All page layouts — verbatim except page.tsx hero block

adaptation_plan.description: Add a prominent provenance badge to EditionCard showing '#N of M' in accent color with larger text, making edition scarcity/number more visible at the browsing stage. Baseline card shows this in 12px text; adaptation promotes it to a featured badge above the title.
adaptation_plan.scope: components/EditionCard.tsx — UI-only change, no engine or spec change
adaptation_plan.expected_kpi_delta: 0.0% (cohort is engine-blind to UI changes; verdict will be GO or ITERATE based on no regression)

ac_coverage.evidence: 12 ACs cover: build, contracts, QA, copy lock, nonclaims, negative proof (code+dep+runtime), pages, golden path.

plan_completeness.evidence: Engineering plan specifies stack, structure, copy overrides, dynamic-segment fix, and what stays verbatim. Adaptation plan scoped to single component.

risk_coverage.evidence: AC8 (code proof) and AC9 (dep proof) cover chainless boundary directly. AC10 covers runtime. Adaptation expected 0.0% delta documented as nonclaim.

measurability.evidence: Every AC is binary pass/fail. Copy ACs are exact string matches. Negative proof ACs are grep results. Golden-path AC is a click sequence.
